---
title: "Bayesian Basket Trial Power simulation study"
author: "Angelo Capasso"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r setup}
source("basket_functions.R")
```


```{r options}
invisible(lapply(pkgs, load_or_install))
options(mc.cores = parallel::detectCores())
```


```{r define-scenarios}
## Panel 1: High Effect size
scenarios_effect08 <- list(
  list(name = "P1: high heterogeneity, effect size = 0.8",     n_baskets = 5,
       n = c(17,15,18,12,23), mu_treat = rep(0.8, 5), sd = 1, scenario_code = "P1"),
  list(name = "P2: low heterogeneity, effect size = 0.8",      n_baskets = 5,
       n = c(17,15,18,12,23), mu_treat = rep(0.8, 5), sd = 1, scenario_code = "P2"),
  list(name = "P3: moderate heterogeneity, effect size = 0.8", n_baskets = 5,
       n = c(17,15,18,12,23), mu_treat = rep(0.8, 5), sd = 1, scenario_code = "P3")
)

## Panel 2: Moderate Effect size
scenarios_effect05 <- list(
  list(name = "P1: high heterogeneity, effect size = 0.5",     n_baskets = 5,
       n = c(17,15,18,12,23), mu_treat = rep(0.5, 5), sd = 1, scenario_code = "P1"),
  list(name = "P2: low heterogeneity, effect size = 0.5",      n_baskets = 5,
       n = c(17,15,18,12,23), mu_treat = rep(0.5, 5), sd = 1, scenario_code = "P2"),
  list(name = "P3: moderate heterogeneity, effect size = 0.5", n_baskets = 5,
       n = c(17,15,18,12,23), mu_treat = rep(0.5, 5), sd = 1, scenario_code = "P3")
)

## Panel 3: Low Effect size
scenarios_effect02 <- list(
  list(name = "P1: high heterogeneity, effect size = 0.2",     n_baskets = 5,
       n = c(17,15,18,12,23), mu_treat = rep(0.2, 5), sd = 1, scenario_code = "P1"),
  list(name = "P2: low heterogeneity, effect size = 0.2",      n_baskets = 5,
       n = c(17,15,18,12,23), mu_treat = rep(0.2, 5), sd = 1, scenario_code = "P2"),
  list(name = "P3: moderate heterogeneity, effect size = 0.2", n_baskets = 5,
       n = c(17,15,18,12,23), mu_treat = rep(0.2, 5), sd = 1, scenario_code = "P3")
)
```


```{r run-simulations}
set.seed(2025)
n_sim_real <- 500

# FIX: run_simulation() ora restituisce list($decisions, $tau_df).
# run_scenarios() estrae entrambi e li conserva separatamente.
run_scenarios <- function(scenarios) {
  map(scenarios, function(sc) {
    sim <- run_simulation(
      n_sim     = n_sim_real,
      n_baskets = sc$n_baskets,
      n         = sc$n,
      mu_treat  = sc$mu_treat,
      sd        = sc$sd,
      scenario  = sc$scenario_code
    )
    list(
      scenario    = sc$name,
      performance = evaluate_performance(sim$decisions),  # FIX: sim$decisions
      tau_df      = sim$tau_df                            # FIX: salva tau_df
    )
  })
}

results_08 <- run_scenarios(scenarios_effect08)
results_05 <- run_scenarios(scenarios_effect05)
results_02 <- run_scenarios(scenarios_effect02)
```
```{r summary-tables}
make_summary_table <- function(results) {
  map_dfr(results, function(x) {
    x$performance %>%
      mutate(scenario = x$scenario)
  })
}

summary_table_08 <- make_summary_table(results_08)
summary_table_05 <- make_summary_table(results_05)
summary_table_02 <- make_summary_table(results_02)
```
```{r tau-credible-intervals}
# FIX: raccoglie tutti i tau_df dei tre panel in un unico dataframe,
# poi stampa il CI al 95% di tau e I² per scenario e effect size.

collect_tau <- function(results, effect_label) {
  map_dfr(results, ~ .x$tau_df) |>
    mutate(effect_size = effect_label)
}

tau_all <- bind_rows(
  collect_tau(results_08, "0.8"),
  collect_tau(results_05, "0.5"),
  collect_tau(results_02, "0.2")
)

# Tabella riassuntiva: tau e I² per scenario x effect size
tau_summary_table <- tau_all |>
  group_by(scenario, effect_size) |>
  summarise(
    tau_mean   = mean(tau),
    tau_lower  = quantile(tau, 0.025),
    tau_upper  = quantile(tau, 0.975),
    I2_mean    = mean(I2) * 100,
    I2_lower   = quantile(I2, 0.025) * 100,
    I2_upper   = quantile(I2, 0.975) * 100,
    .groups    = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    `tau [95% CI]` = sprintf("%.3f [%.3f – %.3f]", tau_mean, tau_lower, tau_upper),
    `I2 [95% CI]`  = sprintf("%.1f%% [%.1f%% – %.1f%%]", I2_mean, I2_lower, I2_upper)
  ) |>
  select(scenario, effect_size, `tau [95% CI]`, `I2 [95% CI]`)

knitr::kable(
  tau_summary_table,
  caption = "Posterior τ and I² (mean and 95% credible interval) by scenario and effect size"
)
```


```{r tau-posterior-plot}
# FIX: grafico della distribuzione posteriore di tau per scenario,
# separato per effect size tramite facet.

ggplot(tau_all, aes(x = tau, fill = scenario, color = scenario)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.35, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(
    data = tau_all |>
      group_by(scenario, effect_size) |>
      summarise(tau_mean = mean(tau), .groups = "drop"),
    aes(xintercept = tau_mean, color = scenario),
    linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.7
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ effect_size, labeller = labeller(
    effect_size = \(x) paste0("Effect size = ", x)
  )) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, NA)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Posterior distribution of τ by scenario and effect size",
    subtitle = "Dashed lines = posterior mean",
    x        = "τ (between-basket SD)",
    y        = "Density",
    fill     = "Scenario",
    color    = "Scenario"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```


```{r plot-power-by-basket}
plot_power_basket <- function(summary_table) {
  summary_table %>%
    filter(!is.nan(power)) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = basket, y = power, color = scenario, group = scenario)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
    geom_point(size = 3) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), labels = scales::percent) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:5) +
    labs(title = "Power by Basket", x = "Basket", y = "Power") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
}

p1_basket_08 <- plot_power_basket(summary_table_08)
p1_basket_05 <- plot_power_basket(summary_table_05)
p1_basket_02 <- plot_power_basket(summary_table_02)

(p1_basket_08 / p1_basket_05 / p1_basket_02) +
  plot_annotation(
    title    = "Basket Trial Results",
    subtitle = sprintf("n_sim = %d", n_sim_real)
  )
```
```{r power-curves}
effect_1 <- rep(0.8, 5)
effect_2 <- rep(0.5, 5)
effect_3 <- rep(0.2, 5)

# FIX: find_power_curve() ora restituisce list($power_curve, $tau_df).
# compute_power_curves() estrae solo $power_curve per il plot.
compute_power_curves <- function(scenario_code) {
  bind_rows(
    find_power_curve(mu_treat = effect_1, scenario_code = scenario_code)$power_curve,
    find_power_curve(mu_treat = effect_2, scenario_code = scenario_code)$power_curve,
    find_power_curve(mu_treat = effect_3, scenario_code = scenario_code)$power_curve
  )
}

power_P1 <- compute_power_curves("P1")
power_P2 <- compute_power_curves("P2")
power_P3 <- compute_power_curves("P3")

target_power <- 0.8

plot_power_curve <- function(power_data, heterogeneity_label) {
  ggplot(power_data, aes(x = n, y = power, color = factor(effect_size))) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
    geom_point(size = 3) +
    facet_grid(~ scenario) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = target_power, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent, limits = c(0, 1)) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Power Curve stratified for different effect sizes",
                    sprintf("(%s heterogeneity)", heterogeneity_label)),
      x     = "Sample Size per Basket",
      y     = "Power",
      color = "Effect size"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
}

plot_power_curve(power_P1, "high")
plot_power_curve(power_P2, "low")
plot_power_curve(power_P3, "moderate")
```
```{r plot-metrics}
plot_metric_basket <- function(summary_table, metric, y_label) {
  summary_table %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = basket, y = !!sym(metric), color = scenario, group = scenario)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
    geom_point(size = 3) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:5) +
    labs(title = paste(y_label, "by Basket"), x = "Basket", y = y_label) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
}

# Power
p_power_08 <- plot_metric_basket(summary_table_08, "power", "Power")
p_power_05 <- plot_metric_basket(summary_table_05, "power", "Power")
p_power_02 <- plot_metric_basket(summary_table_02, "power", "Power")

# MAPE
p_mape_08 <- plot_metric_basket(summary_table_08, "MAPE", "MAPE")
p_mape_05 <- plot_metric_basket(summary_table_05, "MAPE", "MAPE")
p_mape_02 <- plot_metric_basket(summary_table_02, "MAPE", "MAPE")

# Mean CI Width
p_ci_08 <- plot_metric_basket(summary_table_08, "mean_ci_width", "Mean CI Width")
p_ci_05 <- plot_metric_basket(summary_table_05, "mean_ci_width", "Mean CI Width")
p_ci_02 <- plot_metric_basket(summary_table_02, "mean_ci_width", "Mean CI Width")

(p_power_08 / p_power_05 / p_power_02) + plot_annotation(title = "Power by Basket – Different Effect Sizes")
(p_mape_08  / p_mape_05  / p_mape_02)  + plot_annotation(title = "MAPE by Basket – Different Effect Sizes")
(p_ci_08    / p_ci_05    / p_ci_02)    + plot_annotation(title = "Mean CI Width by Basket – Different Effect Sizes")
```
